name: Microvast Holdings, Inc.
business: Microvast designs, develops, and manufactures lithium-ion battery components and systems primarily for electric commercial vehicles and energy storage systems (ESS). It sells to OEMs in commercial vehicle, port, mining, and rail sectors, as well as to ESS project developers.

exposures.sentence: We specialize in the design, development, and manufacturing of battery components and systems primarily for electric commercial vehicles and energy storage systems (ESS).
Our technology is currently deployed across a wide variety of platforms, including buses, heavy-duty trucks, port equipment, and heavy mining equipment.
We believe energy storage is now a critical component of grid infrastructure.
This demand is accelerated by rising electricity consumption from AI, high-performance computing, and data center expansion.

As a vertically integrated battery company, Microvast develops and owns proprietary intellectual property and know-how relating to the design, development, and manufacture of the four critical components of lithium-ion batteries: cathode, anode, electrolyte, and separator. This vertical integration allows us to use these components in our products and market the individual components to other manufacturers.
In the port and mining sectors, we have supplied Kalmar Corp., XCMG, and LGMG.
We serve a diverse range of commercial vehicle markets, including: ... Automated guided vehicles and specialty vehicles.
Since 2015, we have been working towards developing a 100% solid-state battery technology that eliminates the need for liquid electrolytes.

catalysts.event: Completion of first prototype vehicles for koda partnership
catalysts.sentence: The first prototype vehicles integrating the jointly developed battery electric multiple units are expected to be completed by the end of 2026 and are anticipated to be deployed starting in 2027.

catalysts.event: Deployment of koda battery electric multiple units
catalysts.sentence: The first prototype vehicles integrating the jointly developed battery electric multiple units are expected to be completed by the end of 2026 and are anticipated to be deployed starting in 2027.
We expect equipment installation to conclude in 2026, followed by commissioning and pilot production.

catalysts.event: First customer deliveries from Clarksville pack line
catalysts.sentence: Customer deliveries are expected from the pack line in 2026.

catalysts.event: Sale of Lake Mary facility closing
catalysts.sentence: The Company currently anticipates that, if completed, the transaction would close during the second quarter of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Global energy storage power capacity grew ~90 GW in 2025, a 23% increase YoY
tamClaims.sentence: In 2025, third-party industry data shows that global power capacity grew by approximately 90 gigawatts, an estimated 23% increase from the previous year.

tamClaims.claim: Average CAGR of 23% in deployed GW from 2025 to 2035
tamClaims.sentence: Industry projections indicate expected further expansion, with an average CAGR in deployed gigawatts of 23% between 2025 and 2035.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. power capacity projected to increase from ~45 GW in 2025 to ~125 GW by 2030
tamClaims.sentence: The U.S. and China are expected to lead this growth, with U.S. power capacity projected to increase from approximately 45 gigawatts in 2025 to approximately 125 gigawatts by 2030.
